# People — 1987

Notable births, deaths, and figures of 1987.

<!-- BEGIN GENERATED: category-index:people — maintained by build-structure; do not edit by hand -->
| Topic | Description |
|---|---|
| [Gary Hart — Withdrawal from Presidential Race (May 8, 1987)](gary-hart.md) | The Democratic frontrunner for the 1988 nomination suspends his campaign after press surveillance exposes an affair, permanently altering how media covers candidates' private lives. |
| [Mathias Rust — Flight to Red Square (May 28, 1987)](mathias-rust.md) | An 18-year-old West German amateur pilot flies a Cessna from Helsinki and lands beside the Kremlin, triggering the largest Soviet military purge since the 1930s. |
| [Klaus Barbie — Convicted of Crimes Against Humanity (July 4, 1987)](klaus-barbie.md) | Former Lyon Gestapo chief is convicted in France's first crimes-against-humanity prosecution and sentenced to life imprisonment for the deportation and murder of thousands. |
| [Rudolf Hess (death, August 17, 1987)](rudolf-hess.md) | Hitler's former deputy dies at 93 in Spandau Prison, ending four-power Allied custody and prompting the prison's demolition. |
<!-- END GENERATED: category-index:people -->
